column1: FCRM - Implement agency-specific Forth FCRM - API integration for agent notifications
column32:
*Description*
Implement support for agency-specific Forth APIs to retrieve assigned agents and their email addresses for the payment reminder project. The solution should allow each participating agency to have its own Forth API credentials and CC email configuration. Forth API calls will be executed sequentially to minimize rate-limit risks, reduce implementation complexity, and simplify operational management. Required AWS configuration and secret management should also be implemented.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Agency-specific Forth API credentials supported-
* -Agency-specific CC email configuration implemented-
* -Agent and email information retrieved using the agency's Forth API-
* -Sequential API call flow implemented to avoid rate-limit issues-
* -Required AWS configuration and secrets management completed-
* -Solution supports onboarding additional agencies without code changes to the core workflow-
* -Implementation validated with at least one agency environment-

column1: FCRM - Investigate and validate a solution for missing Assigned To data in agency-managed clients
column32:
*Description*
Research and validate a solution to retrieve the correct *Assigned To* information for clients managed by internal agencies (e.g., DRL Agency) when this data is not available in the current Snowflake environment. The goal is to determine whether the proposed approach is viable before planning a full implementation, ensuring reminder emails for upcoming and failed payments can be sent to the correct agents.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Current Snowflake data limitations analyzed-
* -Proposed solution researched and tested with real agency-managed clients-
* -Agent identification and email retrieval successfully validated-

*Deliverables*

* Confirmed that the current Snowflake process continues to provide transactions, client information, and related payment data as it does today
* Identified that the Assigned To field is unavailable for clients managed by internal agencies because Snowflake does not store agents outside of Cordoba Legal
* Validated an alternative approach using the agency's Forth API when Assigned To is null:
** Retrieve the contact information using the Forth *Get Contact* endpoint
Extract the assigned user identifier from the contact ([https://developer.setforth.com/api/forth-crm/core-objects/contact/contact-object/get-contact|https://developer.setforth.com/api/forth-crm/core-objects/contact/contact-object/get-contact|smart-link])
** Retrieve the agent information and email using the Forth *Get User* endpoint ([https://developer.setforth.com/api/forth-crm/platform-management/administration/users/get-user|https://developer.setforth.com/api/forth-crm/platform-management/administration/users/get-user])
* Confirmed that the retrieved agent information can be used to populate the Assigned To value and support reminder emails for upcoming and failed payments.

column1: FCRM - Replace IAM User Access Keys with IAM Role for AWS deployments
column32:
*Description*
Update the AWS deployment process to use an *IAM Role* instead of *IAM User Access Keys* for SAM deployments executed through GitHub Actions. This follows AWS security best practices by using temporary credentials and reducing the risks associated with long-lived access keys.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -IAM Role created and configured with the required deployment permissions-
* -GitHub Actions configured to assume the IAM Role using OIDC or the approved authentication mechanism-
* -IAM User Access Keys removed from the deployment workflow-
* -SAM deployments execute successfully using the IAM Role-
* -No hardcoded or long-lived AWS credentials required in GitHub Secrets-

column1: FCRM - Business meeting to evaluate DRL Agency agent assignment data solution
column32:
*Description*
Meet with the business team to review and identify a solution for the issue where Snowflake does not store the *Assigned To* information for clients associated with *DRL Agency*. Since the current Snowflake environment belongs to COrdoba Legal, it does not contain DRL Agency-specific assignment data or agent information. Evaluate possible approaches and their implications for scalability and performance.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Business meeting conducted to review the issue and possible solutions-
* -Current data limitations in Snowflake analyzed -
* -Alternative approaches evaluated and discussed-
* -Technical and business impacts identified for each option-

*Deliverables*

* Confirmed that the current Snowflake instance does not store DRL Agency assignment data or agent information
* Identified two potential solutions:
** Use a dedicated Snowflake instance containing DRL Agency data
** Retrieve assignment information directly from the DRL Agency Forth API
* Documented trade-offs of using the Forth API, including additional latency and dependency on external systems
* Identified scalability concerns, as supporting additional agencies would require access to each agency's Snowflake environment or Forth API

column1: CP - Chirp Admin & Sandbox — Developer Guide
column32:
*Description*

Document how to access and configure the Chirp admin portal for production, local development and sandbox testing. This guide will serve as the reference for any developer onboarding to the bank account update integration, covering admin access, IP whitelisting, API credentials setup, and sandbox usage.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Admin portal URL documented and access request process explained
* IP whitelist configuration steps covered for local, staging, and production environments
* API integration credentials located and mapped to backend {{appsettings}} ({{ApiBaseUrl}}, {{ApiSecret}})
* Sandbox setup documented including Chirp Test Bank credentials
* Known sandbox limitations noted (e.g. {{NOT_RETURNED}} status for account numbers with test bank)
* Document published to Confluence under the Engineering standards space

*Deliverables*

column1: Standard: Git & Release Workflow
column32:
*Description*

Create a team-wide engineering standard that documents the Git branching strategy, commit conventions, merge policies, release automation workflow, and CI/CD pipeline configuration used across all Cordoba Portal repositories. The document serves as the single source of truth for day-to-day feature development and release management, and as the implementation guide when onboarding a new repository.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Branching strategy, naming conventions, and merge rules documented-
* -Conventional Commits and Semantic Versioning standards defined with examples-
* -All GitHub Actions pipelines described: PR Check, PR Title Lint, CI/CD, Release Please, and Sync Develop-
* -Infrastructure prerequisites documented: GitHub App setup and AWS OIDC trust policy configuration-
* -Feature development flow documented step by step-
* -Release flow documented step by step, from {{develop}} to production deploy-
* -Implementation checklist included for onboarding a new repository-

*Deliverables*

[https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SW/pages/492863508/Git+Release+Workflow+Standard|https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SW/pages/492863508/Git+Release+Workflow+Standard|smart-link]

column1: CP - IOS - Spike: Architecture Practice: Rick & Morty App
column32:
*Description*

Build a small demo app from scratch that replicates the architecture of the Cordoba Portal iOS project. Using the Rick & Morty public REST API, implement the full stack — Moya, NetworkManagerActor, Combine, Factory, and SwiftfulRouting — across three screens. The goal is to demonstrate that the patterns studied in Spikes 1–3 can be reproduced independently, without referencing the existing codebase.

[https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AYCvH|https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AYCvH|smart-link] 

[https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CL/pages/482017282/Spike+2+Xcode+SwiftUI+and+iOS+Architecture+Fundamentals|https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CL/pages/482017282/Spike+2+Xcode+SwiftUI+and+iOS+Architecture+Fundamentals|smart-link] 

[https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AQD_H|https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AQD_H|smart-link] 

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -App compiles and runs successfully with all dependencies installed-
* -Networking layer mirrors the project architecture — Moya, actor, Combine service layer-
* -At least three screens implemented with navigation between them-
* -Each screen follows the MVVM pattern with protocol-backed ViewModels and Factory injection-
* -Loading, success, and error states handled visually in every screen-

*Deliverables*

[https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CL/pages/491290625/Spike+4+Architecture+Practice+Rick+Morty+App|https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CL/pages/491290625/Spike+4+Architecture+Practice+Rick+Morty+App|smart-link] 

[^RickAndMortyApp.zip]

*Note on CharacterDetailView states:* CharacterDetailView does not implement loading or error states. This is intentional 'cause the screen receives a fully populated {{CharacterModel}} passed as a navigation parameter from CharacterListView, so no additional network call is made. There is no asynchronous operation to represent.

column1: Document CouchDB maintenance runbook
column32:
Document all CouchDB maintenance procedures discovered during the storage investigation so any team member can perform them without tribal knowledge.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Runbook covers all common maintenance tasks
* Stored in Confluence and accessible to all engineers
* Reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer

*Tasks:*

Document the following procedures:

* How to check storage usage (API + filesystem)
* How to run manual compaction (single DB and all DBs)
* How to identify top storage consumers
* How to delete old MODB databases safely
* How to add a CouchDB admin user
* How to interpret {{_active_tasks}}
* How to respond to disk usage alerts
* Kazoo server topology (proxy, logic, db nodes)

column1: SPIKE: Analyze CouchDB storage growth patterns and retention policy
column32:
*Description:* Investigate the current storage growth rate of CouchDB across all accounts and monthly databases (MODBs). Understand how fast storage is growing, which accounts consume the most space, and what a safe retention policy would look like.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Document current total storage usage (databases + view indexes)
* -Identify top 20 accounts by storage consumption-
* Calculate average monthly growth rate per account
* Define recommended CDR retention period
* Document findings in Confluence

*Tasks:*

* Run storage analysis script across all databases
* Aggregate storage by account ID
* Research Kazoo MODB retention configuration options

column1: CX Android: Implement 401/token expiry interceptor to force re-authentication
column32:
API calls that fail due to an expired session token are not currently handled. An interceptor (or equivalent) should detect token expiry errors from any endpoint response and redirect the user to the login screen, clearing any local session state in the process.



*ACs:*

* -Stop closing the app and start redirecting to Login-
* -A request/response interceptor is implemented that inspects all API endpoint responses-
* -When a response indicates token expiry (e.g. {{401 Unauthorized}} with a token expiry reason), the interceptor triggers the logout flow-
* -All local session state (token, user data, etc.) is cleared upon logout-
* -The user is redirected to the login screen after session state is cleared-
* -Any in-flight or queued requests are cancelled when token expiry is detected-
